topic: Zero-day AI threat detection without code changes

flagged.quote: Consider the encoding-attack subsystem alone: the 11-stage security pipeline that every APIRE API request traverses covers more than 26 distinct encoding attack vectors
flagged.reasoning: The 11-stage pipeline is the FULL technical pipeline (stages 1-11 including Authentication, Encoding Detection, Normalization, Content Safety, AI Threat, Pattern Matching, Data Masking, Provider Routing, Data Unmasking, Response Security, Audit Logging). The encoding-attack subsystem itself runs 13 stages of normalization, not 11. The draft conflates these two distinct pipeline concepts.

flagged.quote: Beyond encoding attacks, the five core AI threat categories—prompt injection, jailbreaking, data exfiltration, social engineering, and model inversion—each contain sub-techniques that evolve continuously.
flagged.reasoning: The passage lists only the 5 core AI threat categories by name. It does not name or describe the 8 advanced categories (Adversarial Attacks, Compliance Violations, IP Theft, Shadow AI Usage, API Security, Content Abuse, Business Logic Attacks, Context Attacks). The statement 'each contain sub-techniques' is unsupported—the corpus describes these as distinct categories, not sub-techniques within the core five.

flagged.quote: The proprietary, closed AI model that powers Layer 2 is never trained on customer data—a foundational architectural decision
flagged.reasoning: The corpus states that Layers 1 and 2 use 'proprietary, closed models — never trained on customer data.' The draft attributes this only to Layer 2. Both layers use proprietary closed models with this guarantee, not just Layer 2.

guardian.notes: Draft is well-aligned with corpus on zero-day defense, semantic analysis, multi-vector correlation, ZRA, deployment model, and regulatory framing. Three minor precision issues: conflation of 11-stage vs. 13-stage pipeline concepts, incomplete naming of advanced threat categories, and attribution of the 'closed AI model' guarantee to Layer 2 only rather than both Layers 1 and 2.
